items.claim: The current repo already supports operator artifacts such as workspace target, context carts, refinement sessions, visual artifacts, planning board, budget snapshot, session board, graph snapshot, RAG status, and pipelines.
Cursor Companion is already the first editor surface and supports start plane, import, gap analysis, planning, focus graph, approval, start run, pause/stop, and refresh.
Current operator work added typed workspace targeting, context carts, requirement refinement, visual artifacts, planning board, provider posture, session board, and pop-out support.
The architecture posture already distinguishes canonical write surface from read-only projections and separates declared artifacts from observed runtime state.
The workspace already has a foundational knowledge base with 1124 documents mapped to domain packs.
Apple HIG emphasizes clear hierarchy and toolbars that expose frequently used commands, navigation, and search, and requires obvious visual differences in toggle states.
Material 3 says transitions should help users understand how the app works, uses container transform for element-to-detail transitions, and recommends quick fades for top-level destination changes.
W3C advises using WCAG 2.2 to maximize future applicability of accessibility efforts.
